title: bupleurum falcatum subsp. dilatatum
intro: Bupleurum falcatum subsp. dilatatum, also known as Chinese Kelp or Dilated Bupleurum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, native to China and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Bupleurum falcatum subsp. dilatatum include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Bupleurum falcatum subsp. dilatatum is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, and can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, including respiratory infections, digestive issues, and skin problems.
